summary: The current build evidence for JPEGImportQuality now lines up across decomp, Procmon, and a Win25H2Clean reversible probe: shell32.dll carries the JPEGImportQuality transcode path, Explorer.EXE queried JPEGImportQuality = 100 during a shell-driven wallpaper apply, the missing baseline restores cleanly, and the current app writes the same 100 state.

setting.name: Wallpaper JPEG Import Quality
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can reduce the quality of JPEG images when you set them as wallpaper. People change this when they want the wallpaper to stay as sharp as possible.
setting.why_people_change_it: People set it to 100 when they notice wallpaper compression artifacts or want the imported wallpaper to match the original image quality more closely.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Raising JPEG import quality can improve wallpaper image fidelity, but it can also increase wallpaper storage size and it still rests on shell runtime evidence rather than a Microsoft-published registry contract.
setting.professional_notes: The repo has a decompiled `TranscodeImage` path that reads HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\JPEGImportQuality, uses 85 when the value is missing, clamps the configured number into the 60..100 range, and passes the final value into the WIC JPEG encoder as ImageQuality. A 2026-03-26 Ghidra headless pass on shell32.dll found the JPEGImportQuality string and exported the xref into the same transcode function family. On the same date, a Win25H2Clean Procmon pass using the Explorer shell verb `setdesktopwallpaper` captured Explorer.EXE querying JPEGImportQuality with Data:100. The reversible VM probe still confirms missing -> 100 -> missing. That gives this record code-side proof, runtime proof, and a clean restore story for the app's current value 100 profile.

allowed_values.label: Observed fallback profile
allowed_values.meaning: When JPEGImportQuality is missing, the current repo decomp path falls back to 85 before encoding the wallpaper JPEG.

allowed_values.label: Observed app max-quality profile
allowed_values.meaning: The current app writes 100, the shell32 transcode path accepts it as the top of the 60..100 clamp range, and Procmon captured Explorer.EXE reading JPEGImportQuality = 100 during a shell-driven wallpaper apply.

targets.notes: The decomp path shows a 60..100 clamp range with a missing-value fallback of 85. Runtime Procmon proof was captured for the value 100 path.

app_current_implementation.notes: Guest-side reversible probe on Win25H2Clean confirmed the current app write and restore cycle for JPEGImportQuality.

windows_defaults.label: Observed fallback baseline
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current builds where JPEGImportQuality is left unset and the wallpaper transcode path falls back to its internal default

states.rationale: Leave JPEGImportQuality unset and let the current wallpaper import path fall back to 85.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the current observed fallback behavior from the repo decomp path, not a primary Microsoft registry contract.

recommended_profiles.label: Observed default
avoid_for: Users who want only Microsoft-published registry surfaces

states.rationale: Use the current observed fallback path by leaving JPEGImportQuality unset.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the current observed wallpaper import baseline.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the sharpest possible imported JPEG wallpaper.
tradeoffs: Leaves the current import fallback in place.
Still stays research-gated because the registry surface is not published by Microsoft.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Observed app write used as the current max-quality wallpaper profile.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want the current app's maximum wallpaper import quality profile.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want only primary-doc-backed shell settings.
tradeoffs: Keeps imported JPEG wallpapers at the top of the observed clamp range.
Still relies on decomp plus VM proof instead of a primary Microsoft registry publication.
recommended_profiles.notes: This is the current app-ready high-quality wallpaper profile on the tested build.

evidence.title: Repo system research notes for wallpaper JPEG import quality
evidence.summary: The repo documents JPEGImportQuality behavior, including a fallback of 85 and a clamp range of 60..100, based on code analysis rather than a primary Microsoft registry publication.

evidence.title: Decompiled wallpaper transcode path for JPEGImportQuality
evidence.summary: The decompiled transcode path reads HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\JPEGImportQuality, falls back to 85 when missing, clamps the number into the 60..100 range, and passes the final value into the JPEG encoder as ImageQuality.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer JPEGImportQuality runtime read
evidence.summary: A reversible Procmon pass on Win25H2Clean used the Explorer shell verb `setdesktopwallpaper` on a JPEG copy and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU/Control Panel/Desktop/JPEGImportQuality with Data:100. The repo now keeps the filtered capture files directly under evidence/files/procmon/jpeg-import-quality-validation-20260326

evidence.title: Our Ghidra decompilation - shell32 JPEGImportQuality transcode path
evidence.summary: A headless Ghidra pass on shell32.dll found the JPEGImportQuality string and exported the xref into FUN_1800bf050, the shell transcode path that handles JPEG wallpaper import quality. That gives direct code-side proof on this build.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes JPEGImportQuality = 100 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Win25H2Clean reversible probe - Wallpaper JPEG import quality
evidence.summary: Guest reversible probe toggled JPEGImportQuality to 100 and restored the missing baseline on HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop, confirming the current app write and rollback cycle.

decision.why: shell32.dll decompiled to the JPEGImportQuality transcode path, Explorer.EXE queried JPEGImportQuality = 100 in a shell-driven Procmon pass on Win25H2Clean, the reversible VM probe confirmed missing -> 100 -> missing, and the current app writes the same value with a clean restore story. That is enough to treat the current value 100 profile as app-ready even though the setting is not published by Microsoft as a registry contract.

open_questions: Can a primary Microsoft source for JPEGImportQuality be captured?
Should this setting be treated only as an advanced wallpaper preference?

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: jpegimportquality-state-100.txt: Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Control Panel\Desktop\JPEGImportQuality Data: 100. shell32-jpegimportquality-ghidra.md: shell32.dll xref for JPEGImportQuality in FUN_1800bf050.
validation_proof.notes: The reversible VM probe still covers the missing -> 100 -> missing cycle. The 2026-03-26 Procmon and Ghidra passes add live shell consumption and current-build code-side proof.
